title: marrubium vulgare var. vaillantii
intro: Marrubium vulgare var. vaillantii, commonly known as bitter marrubium, is a variety of the horehound plant (Marrubium vulgare), a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of bitter marrubium include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Bitter marrubium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been used in herbal remedies to calm the digestive system and relieve respiratory symptoms.
